channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Street light out, 12th June
item.description:
This street lamppost has been not working for weeks. I’m not sure if anyone else has reported it. Our close is very dark because this lamppost is not working.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): High Beeches, North Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Mounds, 12th June
item.description:
Some fencing had recently been added but there are still a number of gaps and where wood has split from posts has left sharp wooden daggers sticking upwards this will cause injury if landed on. Could benefit from the whole of the posts being replaced stronger with no gaps for children to fall between and injure

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Imperial Way, Chislehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Gates, 12th June
item.description:
Gate at the bottom end of the park going towards the stream open outwards so just have to be pushed by younger children and they are out this happened 4 times during the time I was there benefit from a latch across the top to prevent, also the gate that is on the roadside

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Imperial Way, Chislehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead fox (double bagged, left by pavement), 12th June
item.description:
Dead fox found in garden of 31 Stone Park Avenue. I double bagged and left by pavement.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 31, Stone Park Avenue, Eden Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Too much Hedge into the pavement, 12th June
item.description:
Too much Hedge into the footpath.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Manor Way, Barnehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The traffic lights is stuck ok red, 12th June
item.description:
The traffic light is stuck on red. the main road stockwell road is working and the pedestrian lights are working too but just the junction to sidney road
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 77, Stockwell Road, Stockwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Construction trailer I believe used a toilets and a kitchen blocking junction, 12th June
item.description:
This trailer has been been worked on the side of south hill this week directly opposite the junction and it is making it virtually impossible to get through if two cars are coming at the same time This is is dangerous as it blocks the vision of any cars potentially coming in the opposite direction

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): South Hill, Elmstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumping of Black Bin bags, 12th June
item.description:
Lot of bin bags have been dumped in the bush and rubbish now everywhere

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Nightingale Vale, Woolwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Weeds, nettles, overhanging greenery, 12th June
item.description:
The alleyway is very overgrown with weeds, nettles and overhanging greenery. It makes it feel very unwelcoming, narrow and dingy. Everything needs trimming back. Young children will be stung by the nettles growing into the walkway. It’s also always covered in litter and dog mess but I can see someone else has reported this

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Springfield Gardens, Southborough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter on Tabard street, 12th June
item.description:
There are plastic bottles, cans, drink carton, tissue, plastic bag and food packaging on the pavement and road on Tabard Street. It has been there for three days.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 217, Tabard Street, The Borough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Clothes on pavement in front of Recycling bin, 12th June
item.description:
There are clothes on the pavement in front of the Scopes clothes recycling bin. It has been there for four days.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Tabard Street, Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter on Tabard street, 12th June
item.description:
There is plastic bottles, cans, tissue and food packaging on the pavement and road on Tabard street. It has been there for three days.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Tabard Street, Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Glennie Road SE27 outside house number 91 / a fe, 12th June
item.description:
Glennie Road SE27 outside house number 91 / a few yards from Royal Circus junction road hole. Apologies, you have had it marked for repair but for a rather a long time I'd 'suggest'. Although on a residential road it is towards the ‘nasty’ end of the pothole spectrum in my view. Please forward fixmystreet.com pin, and picture – picture taken Wednesday night (ie 10th June) so you might just have repaired it …..thank you for your help.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): West Norwood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipped fridge freezer, 12th June
item.description:
Blocking pavement - looking unsightly

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 18, Station Square, Poverest, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lime bike park - bottles of urine everywhere, 12th June
item.description:
When the lime bike drivers deliver bikes, they empty bottles of urine out on the road. This is right outside our house. It’s a health hazard

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Brookfield Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overflowing bins all over the street, 12th June
item.description:
The bins outside the council flats continue to overflow. Rubbish all over the street, not covered, glass and food everywhere

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Brookfield Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 80 Brondesbury villas, 12th June
item.description:
Various items and bagged waste

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Brondesbury Villas, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Small - Less than one bag

item.title: Brondebsury villas- Kilburn high road to 60 both sides, 12th June
item.description:
Clothes, rubbish, laughing gas…

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Brondesbury Villas, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Contractors not cleared up site, 12th June
item.description:
Clearly some contractors have been in the woods clearing the drainage channel from the pond but in doing so have actually blocked any drainage and left all the dug up mud in huge piles within the woods. It’s an absolute eyesore and a really shoddy job. Has this been inspected by anyone after the job was done. And if so how was this deemed to be acceptable?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Berwick Crescent, Falconwood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Trip hazard, 12th June
item.description:
Noted this when walking past. It is a trip hazard and looks dangerous.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 140, Longlands Road, Longlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
